text: The aim of this research is to examine the synergy of disciplinary and religious values and their role in enhancing students’ resilience and sense of responsibility at SMP Nidaul Quran in Sarolangun Regency. This qualitative case study applied the methodology of interview, observation, and thematic analysis. The findings indicate that discipline is one of the most crucial elements of nurturing the characters of students, when taught in conjunction with moral education. Therefore, SMP Nidaul Quran succeeds in instilling discipline upon its students to the extent that they are equipped with stronger psychological resilience and also maximally accountable to ensure that they embody the Islamic values. Variables which contributed towards this success were found to be teachers’ leadership and high involvement of parents. The study demonstrated that religious values extended beyond cognitive skills and had a positive impact upon the students’ sense of obligation in terms of enhancing their personal integrity, thereby confirming the power of successful interaction between teachers, parents and religious values that can serve as alternative learning model for other madrasas to reach the promised goal of educating students for life.
